channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden postpones trip to Germany and Angola to monitor Hurricane Milton
item.description: President Joe Biden said Tuesday he is postponing a planned trip to Germany and Angola to stay at the White House to monitor Hurricane Milton, as it spins closer to Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In battleground states, it's not just about winning — but also losing by less
item.description: In a state where every vote matters, both Democratic and Republican campaigns are not only trying to win in counties where they’re strongest, they’re also trying to lose by less.
item.dc:creator: Ari Shapiro, Mia Venkat, Karen Zamora, and Ashley Brown, MPR News

item.title: Biden-Harris admin ‘taking advice from foreign governments’ on policing speech, lawmaker charges
item.description: Senior Biden-Harris administration officials gained advice from U.K. officials about partnering with social media platforms to police "disinformation" content, documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump-backed House candidate rips Biden's 'joke' border policy for inviting gang violence to swing state
item.description: Fox News Digital spoke to AZ GOP congressional candidate Abe Hamadeh and GOP Chairwoman Gina Swoboda who warned that transnational gangs are coming across the border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From kissy-face sitdown on ‘The View’ to a tougher grilling on ‘60 Minutes,’ Kamala Harris is making news
item.description: The mainstream leftist media has given presidential hopeful Kamala Harris a lot of air time in the past few weeks, but their interviews may resemble cheerleading.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military board substantiates misconduct but declines to fire Marine who adopted Afghan orphan
item.description: CAMP LEJEUNE, N.C. (AP) — A U.S. Marine whose adoption of an Afghan war orphan has spurred a yearslong legal battle and raised alarms at the highest levels of government will remain on active duty. A three-member panel of Marines found Tuesday that while Maj.
item.dc:creator: JULIET LINDERMAN, CLAIRE GALOFARO and MARTHA MENDOZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Democrats in close races try to show they hear voter concerns about immigration
item.description: In some of the closest House races in the country, Democratic candidates are leaning into an issue that Republicans have made a centerpiece of their pitch to voters — immigration.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Voters in the US don't directly elect the president. Sometimes that can undermine the popular will
item.description: To voters in many other democracies, the process of electing a president in the U.S. is a head-scratcher.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Early in-person voting begins in Arizona, drawing visits from the presidential campaigns
item.description: Early in-person voting is beginning in Arizona, making it the first of this year’s presidential battleground states where all residents can cast a ballot at a traditional polling place ahead of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Sandoval, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI has arrested an Afghan man who officials say was inspired by the Islamic State militant organization and was plotting an Election Day attack targeting large crowds in the U.S., the Justice Department said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tim Walz calls for elimination of Electoral College at California fundraiser, says 'it needs to go'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz said the Electoral College should be abolished in favor of a national popular vote during a fundraiser in California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden postpones trip to Germany and Angola to monitor Hurricane Milton
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Tuesday he is postponing a planned trip to Germany and Angola to stay at the White House to monitor Hurricane Milton, as it spins closer to Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Harris continues media blitz with 3 more national interviews
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris is continuing her media tour Tuesday with three more interviews for national audiences, with Election Day now just four weeks away.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump secretly sent Putin COVID-19 tests during pandemic shortage, a new book reports
item.description: Vice President Harris is criticizing Trump over the reported shipment, which is detailed in a new book by the journalist Bob Woodward. The president's campaign has denied the reporting.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Trump calls Maine Gov. Janet Mills a man in a mistake-riddled call to supporters, newspaper reports
item.description: BANGOR, Maine (AP) — Republican Donald Trump mixed up Democratic Gov. Janet Mills’ gender on a phone call with supporters in Maine, The Bangor Daily News reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden undermines Harris claim that Ron DeSantis is politicizing hurricane response: 'Doing a great job'
item.description: President Joe Biden praised GOP Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis for being "cooperative" and doing a "great job" in his response to Hurricanes Helene and Milton, despite Vice President Kamala Harris slamming DeSantis for "playing political games.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawsuit seeks to reopen voter registration in Georgia after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Three voting rights groups are asking a federal judge to order the state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November's elections due to Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prospects of a deal with Israel fade away as Saudis invite Iranian foreign minister to visit
item.description: An Israel-Saudi normalization deal has seemingly evaporated as Iran’s foreign minister visits the kingdom on Tuesday to discuss efforts to halt Tel Aviv’s incursions into Gaza and Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Walz appeared on ABC's 'The View' and 'Jimmy Kimmel Live'
item.description: The Democratic presidential ticket is on a media blitz in New York. Vice President Kamala Harris appeared Tuesday on ABC's “The View," and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz appeared on ABC's “Jimmy Kimmel Live” on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Wisconsin Senate race toward GOP
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted the Wisconsin Senate race from "lean Democrat" to "toss up," handing Republican Eric Hovde a boost as he tries to unseat Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.).
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Cruz opponent Allred raises $30 million in 3 months
item.description: Rep. Colin Allred (D-Texas) announced Tuesday that he has raised more than $30 million over the past three months in his battle against incumbent Texas Sen. Ted Cruz (R), reflecting broad desire among Democratic donors to defeat one of the Senate’s most prominent conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: New poll shows Harris taking a slim lead over Trump thanks to support from a surprising group
item.description: Poll indicates increased support from Republicans is helping fuel Vice President Kamala Harris with 4 weeks to go until Election Day in her White House showdown with former President Trump
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court to decide whether Oklahoma must execute death row inmate
item.description: Richard Glossip, an Oklahoma death row inmate, has been scheduled for execution nine times — and each time, he has avoided that fate following legal twists and turns. Convicted for the 1997 killing of his former boss, Glossip has maintained his innocence for nearly three decades.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New poll reveals which voter group are fueling Trump to a narrow edge over Harris in battleground
item.description: Former President Donald Trump holds a razor-thin two-point edge over Vice President Kamala Harris in battleground Arizona, according to a new public opinion poll.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's shifting abortion stance raises questions for evangelical support
item.description: Former President Trump’s softening stance on reproductive health has raised questions about his standing with white evangelical voters, a key part of his coalition dating back to 2016.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Texas AG demands Biden-Harris admin help verify citizenship of nearly 500K registered voters
item.description: The Texas Attorney General has written to the Biden-Har­ris administration urging it to provide information on nearly half a million people who have not had their citizenship verified.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Melania Trump waves off 'hand-swatting incident': A 'mere misunderstanding'
item.description: Melania Trump is waving off speculation that she didn't want to hold her husband's hand, chalking up images of her seemingly "swatting" away former President Donald Trump to a "mere misunderstanding.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: How is Mayor Adams connected to the latest federal indictment?
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- In charging Mohamed Bahi with witness tampering and destroying records, federal authorities have shed light on the separate case against Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris leads Trump among California Latino voters by 23 points
item.description: Vice President Harris holds a strong lead over former President Trump among California Hispanics, according to a Telemundo poll released Tuesday. The California survey contrasts with a similar poll released earlier this month of Florida Hispanic voters, which found Trump leading Harris.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, Fox 5

item.title: Pro-Trump Christian nationalists are on tour to recruit election workers
item.description: “Are you guys ready to show the world that Christians will be silent no more?” said one speaker, whose nonprofit is recruiting people to become election workers.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Hagen and Audrey Nguyen, MPR News

item.title: Johnson sidesteps question on whether Trump lost 2020 election: ‘This is a gotcha game’
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday sidestepped a question about whether former President Trump lost the 2020 presidential election, calling it a “gotcha game” that he would not play.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Major North Carolina newspaper knocks Trump over Helene response 'falsehoods'
item.description: A leading newspaper in North Carolina is blasting former President Donald Trump over what it calls his "falsehoods" about the government response to Hurricane Helene, which devastated large swaths of the state last week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: New analysis suggests national debt could increase under Harris, but it would surge under Trump
item.description: No one is likely to be happy with the projected higher deficits laid out in a new analysis of Kamala Harris' and Donald Trump's economic plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Deputy Mayor for Public Safety Philip Banks resigns
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Deputy Mayor for Public Safety Philip Banks III has resigned from his position, his spokespeople said on Monday. The resignation comes after sources said Friday that First Deputy Mayor Sheena Wright was also expected to resign.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's plan would add $4 trillion more to U.S. debt compared to Harris', analysis says
item.description: Neither former President Donald Trump nor Vice President Kamala Harris have plans to fix the national debt, risking "an eventual fiscal crisis," a budget nonprofit group found in its analysis.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News
